name: opla-ssg
documentation:
# OPLA: One Page Layout Automator

[![pipeline status](https://gitlab.math.unistra.fr/irma/opla/badges/main/pipeline.svg)](https://gitlab.math.unistra.fr/irma/opla/-/commits/main)
[![coverage report](https://gitlab.math.unistra.fr/irma/opla/badges/main/coverage.svg)](https://irma.pages.math.unistra.fr/opla/coverage)
[![Latest Release](https://gitlab.math.unistra.fr/irma/opla/-/badges/release.svg)](https://gitlab.math.unistra.fr/irma/opla/-/releases)
[![pypi package](https://badge.fury.io/py/opla-ssg.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/opla-ssg)
[![Doc](https://img.shields.io/badge/doc-sphinx-blue)](https://irma.pages.math.unistra.fr/opla)

A professional webpage generator with a focus on research activities.

## Features

- Generate a professional webpage from a single markdown file
- Handle publication lists coming from HAL database or from a bibtex file
- Highly customizable thanks to the use of jinja templates, shortcodes and custom styles and scripts

## Example

Here is a preview of a generated page:

![Screenshot of a webpage for Dalembert](dalembert.png)

See complete examples with various graphical themes in this [doc section](https://irma.pages.math.unistra.fr/opla/usage.html#select-your-graphic-theme).

modules.name: opla.shortcodes
documentation: Handle shortcodes for generating publications list from HAL and from BibTeX files

functions.name: opla.shortcodes.publications_handler_hal
documentation:
Generate a list of publications sorted by the document type

Args:
    _: unused positional argument
    kwargs: keyword arguments
    publications: list of publications, None by default

Returns:
    str: The list of the selected type of document publications

functions.name: opla.shortcodes.publications_handler_bibtex
documentation:
Generate a table of publications from a bibtex file

Args:
    _: unused positional argument
    kwargs: keyword arguments
    __: unused context

Returns:
    str: the list of the publications

modules.name: opla.parse
documentation: Parse markdown file into header and sections and create menu from sections

functions.name: opla.parse.parse_file
documentation:
Parse a markdown file into a config dict and a list of sections

Args:
    mdfile_path: Path to the markdown file

Returns:
    (config of the markdown file, content sections of the markdown file, menu items)

functions.name: opla.parse.process_favicon
documentation:
Check if favicon is present in the config dict

Args:
    config: opla config dict

functions.name: opla.parse.process_theme
documentation:
Process the theme section of the config dict

Args:
    config: opla config dict

functions.name: opla.parse.get_custom_static_files
documentation:
Add css and js file paths to config["theme"]["custom"] dict

Args:
    config: opla config dict

functions.name: opla.parse.process_footer
documentation:
Process the footer section of the config dict

Args:
    config: opla config dict

functions.name: opla.parse.process_config
documentation:
Process the opla config dict

Args:
    config: opla config dict

functions.name: opla.parse.create_menu
documentation:
Create a menu from a collection of sections

Args:
    sections: Sections of the markdown with an id and a title

Returns:
    A list of menu items with a link href and a text

modules.name: opla.templating
documentation: Provide a function to get the jinja template.

functions.name: opla.templating.get_template
documentation:
Get the appropriate template according to the theme configuration

Args:
    config: the site configuration dictionary

Returns:
    jinja2.Template: the requested template

modules.name: opla.payload
documentation: Provide functions to copy static and attached files to output directory

functions.name: opla.payload.copy
documentation:
Copy a file or a directory tree to a destination directory

Args:
    src: path to the source file or directory
    dst: path to the destination directory

functions.name: opla.payload.copy_custom_static_files
documentation:
Copy custom files to a destination folder

Args:
    config: opla config dict
    dst_dir: path to the destination folder

functions.name: opla.payload.copy_static_files
documentation:
Copy static files (if they exist) to a destination folder

Args:
    config: opla config dict
    dst_dir: path to the destination folder

functions.name: opla.payload.copy_data_files
documentation:
Copy data files to a destination folder

Args:
    config: opla config dict
    dst_dir: path to the destination folder

functions.name: opla.payload.copy_payload
documentation:
Copy static and attached files to a destination folder

Args:
    config: opla config dict
    dst_dir: path to the destination folder

functions.name: opla.payload.create_output_directory
documentation:
Create the output directory. If it already exists, delete it and create a new one

Args:
    dir: path to the output directory

Returns:
    Path: the created output directory

modules.name: opla
documentation: A professional webpage generator with a focus on research activities.

modules.name: opla.opla
documentation: Main module for the opla package

functions.name: opla.opla.get_parser
documentation:
Return the parser for the opla command-line interface

Returns:
    argparse.ArgumentParser: the parser for the opla command-line interface

functions.name: opla.opla.main
documentation:
Generates a personal page by parsing command-line arguments,
creating the page content and its menu, renders the HTML template,
and writes the result into a HTML file

modules.name: opla.__main__
documentation: Execute OPLA as a module

modules.name: opla.bibliography
documentation: Retrieve publications from HAL and handle publications list style format

functions.name: opla.bibliography.get_publications
documentation:
Get the list of publications sorted by the document type, based on
the IDHal of the author by questionning HAL

Args:
    idhal: The id of the author

Returns:
    dict: A dictionnary containing the publications retrieved
              from the HAL API sorted by document type

classes.name: opla.bibliography.MyBackend
documentation: Output the entry in a markdown list

classes.name: opla.bibliography.MySortingStyle
documentation: Sort entries by year in descending order

classes.name: opla.bibliography.MyStyle

methods.name: opla.bibliography.MyStyle.format_web_refs
documentation: Add HAL ref based on urlbst output.web.refs

methods.name: opla.bibliography.MyStyle.format_idhal
documentation: Format HAL ref based on urlbst format.doi
A custom class to display the HAL reference
